channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Built a Smart Gemini API Key Manager Because Rate Limits Were Driving Me Crazy

item.description: I kept repeating the same painful cycle: build something in Cursor, then spend 2-3 hours copy-pasting Dockerfiles, debugging nginx SSL configs, and fixing GitHub Actions YAML before I could...

item.title: Why Your RAG System Costs 10x More Than You Think
item.description: The hidden infrastructure tax of Retrieval-Augmented Generation You've probably heard RAG is the future of LLMs. Retrieval-Augmented Generation lets you ground AI responses in your own data...

item.title: AI Prompt Security: How Real-Time Filtering Stops Data Leaks
item.description: Not long ago, data breaches were mostly associated with malware, exploits, and unauthorized access. Security teams focused on protecting systems, networks, and endpoints. That model is changing....

item.title: Hot-Knowledge-Sharing-Website 开发环境配置指南
item.description: 本指南主要用于本地测试环境的搭建与配置，涵盖数据库、前端和后端的部署说明。 1. 数据库部署 (PostgreSQL) Windows 环境 官方网站: PostgreSQL (请参考官方文档进行安装) 环境变量配置: 将安装目录下的 bin 文件夹（例如 D:\PostgreSQL\bin）添加到系统环境变量 PATH 中。 macOS 环境 安装 PostgreSQL:...

item.title: Angular Change Detection — The Complete Architect Guide
item.description: Most Angular developers use change detection… But very few truly understand how it works internally. Let’s break it down 👇 ** What is Change Detection? ** Change Detection is Angular’s mechanism...

item.title: Self-host Open WebUI + Ollama in production — the config nobody writes about
item.description: Open WebUI's quickstart is great for local dev. One command, it's running. But putting it on a real server for a team requires a lot more — SSL, auth lockdown, WebSocket proxy, backups. Here's the...

item.title: I Spent 3 Months Tuning a Tokio Runtime for My Robot - Here's What No Tutorial Tells You
item.description: Last November, my robot arm started dropping sensor frames at exactly 47ms intervals. Not randomly - exactly 47ms, like clockwork. It would read joint angles perfectly for a while, then miss a...

item.title: Authentication Systems Explained: JWT vs Sessions (What You Should Actually Use in 2026)
item.description: Authentication is one of those topics every developer uses—but not everyone truly understands. At some point, you’ll face this decision: 👉 Should I use JWT (JSON Web Tokens) or Session-based...

item.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability...

item.title: Operation PowerOFF Seizes 53 DDoS Domains, Exposes 3 Million Criminal Accounts
item.description: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more...

item.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To...

item.title: YouTube’s mobile app finally lets you share timestamped videos
item.description: YouTube is making some changes that might affect how you share videos from the mobile app. From the app, you can finally share videos from a specific timestamp, which will make it easier to point...

item.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
item.description: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

item.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
item.description: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.

item.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
item.description: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

item.title: Gucci-branded Google smart glasses are coming next year
item.description: Google is reportedly partnering with Gucci to make a pair of AI smart glasses stylish enough people might actually want to wear them. According to Reuters, Gucci parent company Kering is planning...

item.title: Ballmer gives $80 million to NPR, with strings attached
item.description: Connie Ballmer, wife of former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer and cofounder of the Ballmer Group, has given $80 million to NPR. That's roughly seven years' worth of government funding ($11.2m) after...

item.title: Netflix embraces vertical video with major mobile app update
item.description: Netflix announced on Thursday that it will be launching a redesigned mobile app, which will include a vertical video feed, at the end of April. "This redesign will better reflect our expanding...

item.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
item.description: The new model, called π0.7, represents what the company describes as an early but meaningful step toward the long-sought goal of a general-purpose robot brain.

item.title: Netflix cofounder Reed Hastings is officially leaving the company
item.description: Netflix cofounder and chairman Reed Hastings plans to leave the company after nearly 30 years. The news comes as part of Netflix's Q1 2026 earnings results released on Thursday, which says...

item.title: OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
item.description: OpenAI's agentic coding tool has gotten a major makeover, with a variety of new powers and abilities.

item.title: Live Nation says it will fight monopoly suit loss
item.description: After a jury found that Live Nation-Ticketmaster violated antitrust law on several counts, the company warns in a blog post that the verdict "is not the last word on this matter." The company...

item.title: Ozlo’s comfy Sleepbuds are nearly 30 percent off in the run-up to Mother’s Day
item.description: If you’re struggling to get a full night of rest, a good pair of sleep earbuds can help you tune out unwanted distractions. The Ozlo Sleepbuds are one such option, and they’re currently available...

item.title: Teenage Engineering might be getting into instrument amps next
item.description: An unannounced Teenage Engineering device, the KO-Amp 35, can be found over at the FCC in a new filing. The label clearly marks it as a member of the mid-range EP family instruments, which...

item.title: The only way to fight deepfakes is by making deepfakes
item.description: I was unsure if my parents would notice that the voice on the other end wasn't mine - or that it was mine, sort of, but it wasn't me. The voice said hello, asked my dad how he was doing, and asked...

item.title: Casely has reannounced a power bank recall from 2025 following a fatality
item.description: Casely first recalled over 429,000 of its 5,000mAh Power Pods wireless power banks in April 2025 following 51 reports of their lithium-ion batteries "overheating, expanding or catching fire,"...

item.title: Anthropic CPO leaves Figma’s board after reports he will offer a competing product
item.description: Krieger's departure and any forthcoming design tools will be another data point for investors who fear the SaaSpocalypse — that the largest AI labs will come to dominate software businesses, a...

item.title: The Battle for OpenAI’s Soul
item.description: In Musk v. Altman, a jury will soon determine whether OpenAI has strayed from its founding mission to ensure AGI benefits humanity. Here’s what to know.
